# pythonSoftIOC

{{ code_ci }} {{ docs_ci }} {{ coverage }} {{ pypi_version }} {{ license }}

This module allows an EPICS IOC with Python Device Support to be run from within
the Python interpreter. Records can be programmatically created and arbitrary
Python code run to update them and respond to caputs. It supports cothread and
asyncio for concurrency. PVs are served over Channel Access and PVAccess.

```




PyPI

pip install softioc

Source code

https://github.com/dls-controls/pythonSoftIOC

Documentation

https://dls-controls.github.io/pythonSoftIOC

Changelog

https://github.com/dls-controls/pythonSoftIOC/blob/master/CHANGELOG.rst
```

A simple example of the use of this library:

```python
# Import the basic framework components.
from softioc import softioc, builder
import cothread

# Set the record prefix
builder.SetDeviceName("MY-DEVICE-PREFIX")

# Create some records
ai = builder.aIn('AI', initial_value=5)
ao = builder.aOut('AO', initial_value=12.45, on_update=lambda v: ai.set(v))

# Boilerplate get the IOC started
builder.LoadDatabase()
softioc.iocInit()

# Start processes required to be run after iocInit
def update():
    while True:
        ai.set(ai.get() + 1)
        cothread.Sleep(1)


cothread.Spawn(update)

# Finally leave the IOC running with an interactive shell.
softioc.interactive_ioc(globals())
```

% Anything below this line is used when viewing README.rst and will be replaced
% when included in index.rst

See <https://dls-controls.github.io/pythonSoftIOC> for more detailed documentation.

Device support files for pythonSoftIoc Python EPICS device support.

Note that these definitions don't strictly need to be loaded through the
iocbuilder in this way, but it makes structural sense this way.

methods.name: softioc.pythonSoftIoc.RecordWrapper.__getattr__

From the outside it looks like a builder record until the database is
written.

methods.name: softioc.asyncio_dispatcher.AsyncioDispatcher.__init__
documentation:
A dispatcher for `asyncio` based IOCs, suitable to be passed to
`softioc.iocInit`. Means that `on_update` callback functions can be
async.

If a `loop` is provided it must already be running. Otherwise a new
Event Loop will be created and run in a dedicated thread.

functions.name: tests.test_record_values.record_values
documentation:
A list of parameters for record value setting/getting tests.

Fields are:
- Record name
- Record builder function
- Input value passed to .set()/initial_value/caput
- Expected output value after doing .get()/caget
- Expected type of the output value from .get()/caget

Run the test function using multiprocessing and check returned value is

set and how the value is retrieved, respectively.

modules.name: softioc
documentation: Python soft IOC module.

methods.name: softioc.cothread_dispatcher.CothreadDispatcher.__init__
documentation:
A dispatcher for `cothread` based IOCs, suitable to be passed to
`softioc.iocInit`.  By default scheduled tasks are run on a dedicated
cothread callback thread, but an alternative dispatcher can optionally
be specified here.  Realistically the only sensible alternative is to
pass `cothread.Spawn`, which would create a separate cothread for each
dispatched callback.

functions.name: softioc.device_core.with_metaclass
documentation: Black magic lifted from six.py (http://pypi.python.org/pypi/six/) to replace
use of __metaclass__ for metaclass definition

classes.name: softioc.device_core.DeviceCommon

methods.name: softioc.device_core.DeviceCommon.__init__
documentation: By requiring that DeviceCommon be a common base class for the entire
Python device hierarchy, we can use this __init__ to test for unused
keyword arguments.

Adds support for an __init_class__ method called when the class or any
of its subclasses is constructed.  Also adds auto-super functionality
(see iocbuilder.support.autosuper).

classes.name: softioc.device_core.DSET_BASE
documentation: Basic dset template used for all record types.  Note that the process
method isn't generic to all records, so isn't included here.

classes.name: softioc.device_core.DeviceSupportCore

methods.name: softioc.device_core.DeviceSupportCore.__init_class__
documentation:
Record support initialisation, called once during class
initialisation for each sub-class.  This registers record support for
the specified device name.
Generic support for binding subclasses of this class to the EPICS
device support layer.  For each registered record type a dset is
constructed containing callbacks into this class.  All callbacks which
are record specific (apart from init_record which needs special
treatment) are then bound to the appropriate class instance and the
appropriate method is invoked.

methods.name: softioc.device_core.DeviceSupportCore.__create_method
documentation: Creates a callable function that can be set as a dset callback.

methods.name: softioc.device_core.DeviceSupportCore.__call_instance_method
documentation:
This is called directly from EPICS as part of record processing.
At this point we are bound to the class (through the closure created
by __create_method() above), but not to the instance.  We find the
instance in the record and dispatch to the bound instance method here.

methods.name: softioc.device_core.DeviceSupportCore._init
documentation:
This routine is called by the record support layer during both
before and after device initialisation.
Device support: implementations of the generic dset entries.

methods.name: softioc.device_core.DeviceSupportCore._init_record
documentation:
This is called for each new record.  We bind the record to its
instance and call any local initialisation.

methods.name: softioc.device_core.DeviceSupportCore.init_record
documentation:
Default record initialisation finalisation.  This can be overridden
to provide a different return value, or to perform further work on
record initialisation.

methods.name: softioc.device_core.DeviceSupportCore._get_ioinit_info
documentation: Implements support for I/O Intr scan type.

methods.name: softioc.device_core.DeviceSupportCore.process_severity
documentation: Support routine to implement alarm processing.
General shared class support.

methods.name: softioc.device_core.DeviceSupportCore.trigger
documentation: Call this to trigger processing for records with I/O Intr scan.

Implementation of core record device support including registration
with EPICS of the device support table.

methods.name: softioc.device_core.RecordLookup.LookupRecord

Returns the registered record with given name, or raises KeyError

This class implements automatic record registration.

functions.name: softioc.softioc.epicsAtPyExit
documentation: tie in epicsAtExit() to interpreter lifecycle

functions.name: softioc.softioc.iocInit
documentation:
This must be called exactly once after loading all EPICS database files.
After this point the EPICS IOC is running and serving PVs.

Args:
: dispatcher: A callable with signature `dispatcher(func, *args)`. Will

  : be called in response to caput on a record. If not supplied use
    `cothread` as a dispatcher.

See Also:

: `softioc.asyncio_dispatcher` is a dispatcher for `asyncio` applications

functions.name: softioc.softioc.safeEpicsExit
documentation: Calls epicsExit() after ensuring Python exit handlers called.

functions.name: softioc.softioc.interactive_ioc
documentation:
Fires up the interactive IOC prompt with the given context.

Args:
    context: A dictionary of values that will be made available to the
        interactive Python shell together with a number of EPICS test
        functions
    call_exit: If `True`, the IOC will be terminated by calling epicsExit
        which means that `interactive_ioc` will not return

classes.name: softioc.device.ProcessDeviceSupportCore

methods.name: softioc.device.ProcessDeviceSupportCore._value_to_epics
documentation: Most subclasses (all except waveforms) define a ctypes constructor for the
underlying EPICS compatible value.

methods.name: softioc.device.ProcessDeviceSupportCore._read_value
documentation: This method is called during Out record processing to return the
underlying value in EPICS format.

methods.name: softioc.device.ProcessDeviceSupportCore._write_value
documentation: This method is called during In record processing to update the
underlying value (the value must be in EPICS compatible format).  This is
also called during Out record initialisation and value reversion when
required.

methods.name: softioc.device.ProcessDeviceSupportCore.get_field
documentation: Returns the given field value as a string.

methods.name: softioc.device.ProcessDeviceSupportCore.set_field
documentation:
Sets the given field to the given value. Value will be transported as
a DBF_STRING.

Implements canonical default processing for records with a _process
method.  Processing typically either copies a locally set value into the
record, or else reads a value from the record and triggers an update.

methods.name: softioc.device.ProcessDeviceSupportIn.set
documentation:
Updates the stored value and triggers an update.  The alarm
severity and timestamp can also be specified if appropriate.

methods.name: softioc.device.ProcessDeviceSupportIn.set_alarm
documentation:
Updates the alarm status without changing the stored value.  An
update is triggered, and a timestamp can optionally be specified.

methods.name: softioc.device.ProcessDeviceSupportIn.get
documentation: Returns the last written value.

methods.name: softioc.device.ProcessDeviceSupportOut.init_record
documentation:
Special record initialisation for out records only: implements
special record initialisation if an initial value has been specified,
allowing out records to have a sensible initial value.

methods.name: softioc.device.ProcessDeviceSupportOut.__completion
documentation: Signals that all on_update processing is finished

methods.name: softioc.device.ProcessDeviceSupportOut._process
documentation:
Processing suitable for output records.  Performs immediate value
validation and asynchronous update notification.

methods.name: softioc.device.ProcessDeviceSupportOut.set
documentation: Special routine to set the value directly.

classes.name: softioc.device.ai
documentation: For ai and ao there's no point in supporting RVAL <-> VAL conversion, so
for these we support no conversion directly.

functions.name: softioc.builder._set_in_defaults
documentation: Wrappers for PythonDevice record constructors.
The SCAN field for all input records defaults to I/O Intr.

functions.name: softioc.builder._set_scalar_out_defaults
documentation: For longout and ao we want DRV{L,H} to match {L,H}OPR by default

functions.name: softioc.builder._process_mbb_values
documentation: Converts a list of (option [,severity]) values or tuples into field settings
suitable for mbbi and mbbo records.

functions.name: softioc.builder._get_length
documentation: Helper function for getting 'length' or 'NELM' from arguments

functions.name: softioc.builder._waveform
documentation:
Helper routine for waveform construction.  If a value is given it is
interpreted as an initial value and used to configure length and datatype
(unless these are overridden), otherwise length and datatype must be
specified.

functions.name: softioc.builder.LoadDatabase
documentation:
This should be called after all the builder records have been created,
but before calling iocInit().  The database is loaded into EPICS memory,
ready for operation.
Support routines for builder

functions.name: softioc.builder.ClearRecords
documentation: Delete all created record information, allowing new record creation
